metadata.title: Nicolás Corbal — Design Engineer
metadata.description: Design Engineer. I build digital products where design and code meet.

bio.p1: Design Engineer at <osix>OSIX</osix>, a dev company I co-founded with six classmates from <usc>USC</usc> where I do frontend and product design. I started Computer Engineering in 2022 and three years later we built the company. Since then I haven't stopped — between classes, client projects and personal stuff there's always something going on.
bio.p2: I build products where design and code meet. My role is <role>frontend and product design</role> — websites, apps, technical consulting, whatever the client needs. Nine months full-time and growing. I like being involved in the whole process, from the first idea to production.
bio.p3: I spend most of my day between X and Illustrator. I like when a project feels right from the first click — typography, spacing, transitions, the kind of things you don't notice until they're missing. I believe good products are built by caring about the small details.
bio.stack: My stack is <react>React</react>, <next>Next.js</next>, <ts>TypeScript</ts> and <tailwind>Tailwind</tailwind>. For design I use <figma>Figma</figma>, <illustrator>Illustrator</illustrator>, <photoshop>Photoshop</photoshop> and <aftereffects>After Effects</aftereffects>. I like tools that stay out of the way — that let you think about the product, not the configuration.
bio.p4: I follow studios like <lusion>Lusion</lusion> and <linear>Linear</linear> closely. Detail-oriented, fast learner, team player. The difference between good and obvious is usually the last 10 percent, and that's the part that motivates me most.
bio.p5: Outside of work I draw inspiration from a lot of things — <music>electronic music</music>, <films>cinema</films> and <books>reading</books>. I consume quite a bit of everything and like to mix references. I think what you see and listen to outside of work ends up showing in what you design, even if you don't realize it.
bio.p7: Always open to new projects and conversations — <link>get in touch</link>.
